search_queries_used: site:auanet.org 2025 microhematuria guideline initial evaluation follow-up PDF
site:auanet.org microhematuria blood pressure creatinine smoking anticoagulant gross hematuria
candidate_sources_rejected: cancer referral summaries without exact recommendation review
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: the AUA/SUFU source concerns adult microhaematuria while the legacy workflow includes visible and microscopic haematuria without age restriction.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for selected initial evaluation and follow-up documentation, not automatic imaging, cystoscopy, referral, diagnosis, or treatment.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE haematuria guideline was identified. AUA/SUFU risk and service pathways require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The official 2025 AUA/SUFU unabridged guideline was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: The 2025 amendment is current and supersedes the unamended 2020 version for this review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped microhaematuria, urinalysis, and microscopy terminology without assigning cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urinalysis with microscopic confirmation and microhaematuria terminology without assigning cause.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped gross or microscopic history, urinary symptoms, anticoagulant, smoking, occupational, family, UTI, flank-pain, blood-pressure, creatinine, and combined-history fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports history, physical examination, blood pressure, creatinine, gross-haematuria history, irritative urinary symptoms, smoking and occupational exposure, family history, anticoagulant context, urinary infection context, and flank-pain context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped recurrent gross-haematuria prompt, safety-net discussion, and clinician-arranged follow-up without automatic investigation or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician shared-decision follow-up and reassessment when gross haematuria or new urinary symptoms occur, without automatic investigation or referral output.

unresolved_source_gaps: The broad workflow does not encode adult, microscopic, risk-stratum, post-infection, renal-disease, or prior-negative-evaluation qualifiers.

Urinary retention, fever, abdominal or flank examination, general vitals, clots, painless visible haematuria, weight loss, imaging, previous records, referral, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up intervals, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Cause, cancer risk, diagnosis, imaging, cystoscopy, referral, treatment, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
